titulo: Multi-Agent Code-Orchestrated Generation for Reliable Infrastructure-as-Code
formato_armazenamento: Memory Curator stores verified Infrastructure-as-Code tuples (P,T,Pi) with metadata, indexes typed motifs in a symbolic catalog and dense store, and returns typed motifs rather than raw code text for later planning and compilation.
framework_agente: MACOG multi-agent IaC generation architecture with Architect, Provider Harmonizer, Engineer, Reviewer, Security Prover, Cost and Capacity Planner, DevOps, and Memory Curator
llm_utilizado: GPT-5, Gemini-2.5 Pro, GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo, Gemini 2.0 Flash, Magicoder, WizardCoder, CodeLlama
benchmarks_utilizados: IaC-Eval
metricas_desempenho: IaC-Eval score, BLEU, CodeBERTScore, LLM-judge score, ablation scores
baseline_comparado: Few-shot, chain-of-thought, multi-turn, RAG, and MACOG ablations without specialized agents/components.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Verified tuples and typed motifs can be accumulated in symbolic and dense stores; no growth curve or memory size limit is reported.
custo_por_tarefa: NR
resultado_principal: MACOG improves IaC-Eval over prompt-only and RAG baselines. GPT-5 improves from 54.90 with RAG to 74.02 with MACOG, and Gemini-2.5 Pro improves from 43.56 with RAG to 60.13 with MACOG.
desempenho_vs_baseline: GPT-5 MACOG reaches 74.02 IaC-Eval, BLEU 11.86, CodeBERTScore 80.54, and LLM-judge 94.10. Ablations reduce IaC-Eval, including -Engineer at 64.89, -Security Prover at 61.45, -DevOps at 56.93, and -Memory Curator at 72.17.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; system includes a Cost and Capacity Planner but no extracted per-task monetary cost is reported.
limitacoes: Evaluation is focused on Terraform/IaC generation; no source release or monetary/token cost data captured; memory-curator contribution is positive but smaller than some other component ablations.
ameacas_validade: IaC-Eval benchmark design, synthetic/task distribution, LLM-judge evaluation, Terraform/provider versioning, and multi-agent orchestration choices may affect external validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes ReasoningBank as a memory framework that distills successful and failed experiences into reusable reasoning strategies, retrieves them for later tasks, and consolidates new experiences back into memory across web and software-engineering benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 006; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Siru Ouyang, Jun Yan, I-Hung Hsu, Yanfei Chen, Ke Jiang, Zifeng Wang, Rujun Han, Long T. Le, Samira Daruki, Xiangru Tang, Vishy Tirumalashetty, George Lee, Mahsan Rofouei, Hangfei Lin, Jiawei Han, Chen-Yu Lee, Tomas Pfister
titulo: ReasoningBank: Scaling Agent Self-Evolving with Reasoning Memory
formato_armazenamento: Reasoning memory items distilled from self-judged successful and failed trajectories, with structured fields such as title, description, and when-to-apply guidance; new experiences are consolidated back into the repository.
framework_agente: ReasoningBank with memory-aware test-time scaling (MaTTS)
benchmarks_utilizados: WebArena, Mind2Web, SWE-bench Verified
metricas_desempenho: Resolve rate, average interaction steps, benchmark success metrics, ablation scores
baseline_comparado: No Memory, Synapse, AWM and success-only/failure-trajectory ablations
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory grows as successful and failed experiences are distilled and consolidated after tasks; MaTTS scales interaction experience to synthesize higher-quality memory.
custo_por_tarefa: NR
resultado_principal: ReasoningBank improves across web and software-engineering benchmarks. On SWE-bench Verified, Gemini-2.5-flash improves from 34.2% No Memory to 38.8%, and Gemini-2.5-pro improves from 54.0% to 57.4%, with fewer average interaction steps.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE-bench Verified, Gemini-2.5-flash ReasoningBank reports 38.8% resolve versus 34.2% No Memory and 35.4% Synapse; Gemini-2.5-pro reports 57.4% versus 54.0% No Memory and 53.4% Synapse. Failure-trajectory ablations report larger gains than success-only memory variants.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports efficiency as fewer interaction steps: average 2.8 fewer steps than No Memory and 1.3 fewer than other memory baselines on SWE-bench Verified; no monetary cost extracted.
limitacoes: Memory extraction and success/failure labeling rely on LLM judgments; software-engineering evidence centers on SWE-bench Verified; monetary cost and detailed memory-size scaling are not extracted here.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark mix, LLM-as-judge reliability, transfer from web to software-engineering tasks, model-family dependence, and reproduction of comparator memory mechanisms may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes RedCodeAgent maintaining an adaptive memory of successful red-teaming experiences, storing trajectories, final evaluation results, and self-reflections, then retrieving similar entries for future red-teaming tasks against code agents, with broad attack-success evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 006; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Chengquan Guo, Chulin Xie, Yu Yang, Zhaorun Chen, Zinan Lin, Xander Davies, Yarin Gal, Dawn Song, Bo Li
titulo: RedCodeAgent: Automatic Red-teaming Agent against Diverse Code Agents
formato_armazenamento: Adaptive memory entries for successful attacks store risk scenario, risk description, attack trajectory, final evaluation result, and final self-reflection; memory starts empty and successful attacks are logged for later tasks.
framework_agente: RedCodeAgent automated red-teaming agent
llm_utilizado: GPT-4o-mini base LLM; embedding model for memory retrieval
benchmarks_utilizados: RedCode-Exec, RedCode-Gen, RMCbench; 27 risk scenarios and 810 test cases across code agents and languages
metricas_desempenho: Attack Success Rate (ASR), Refusal Rate (RR), language/task/agent-specific ASR and RR
baseline_comparado: No Jailbreak, GCG, AmpleGCG, Advprompter, AutoDAN, retry variants, and multiple target code agents such as OCI, RA, Cursor, Codeium, and MetaGPT
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory starts empty and accumulates successful red-teaming cases sequentially; top-K retrieval uses similarity with difficulty and trajectory-length penalties.
custo_por_tarefa: NR
resultado_principal: RedCodeAgent achieves the highest ASR and lowest RR across reported benchmark/code-agent combinations. For example, on OCI RedCode-Exec it reports 72.47% ASR and 7.53% RR, and on RA RedCode-Gen it reports 81.52% ASR and 2.50% RR.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Compared with no-jailbreak and jailbreak baselines, RedCodeAgent reports higher ASR across RedCode-Exec, RedCode-Gen, and RMCbench. In selected subtasks it reaches 70.00% ASR/20.00% RR for deleting sensitive files and 93.33% ASR/6.67% RR for adding risky aliases.
custo_vs_baseline: NR
limitacoes: Security red-teaming focus rather than general coding assistance; memory accumulates successful attacks and may depend on scenario sequencing; no monetary/token cost data captured.
ameacas_validade: Sandbox simulation, benchmark scenario selection, target-agent mix, LLM-based planning, dynamic tool selection, and attack-success definitions may affect external validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes Reflection-Driven Control with a reflective memory repository that writes new cases back to memory, continuously evolves accumulated knowledge, and evaluates security-oriented code generation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 006; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Bin Wang, Jiazheng Quan, Xingrui Yu, Hansen Hu, Yuhao, Ivor Tsang
titulo: Reflection-Driven Control for Trustworthy Code Agents
formato_armazenamento: Evolving reflective memory repository with dynamic memory for newly safe/unsafe cases and structured reflection records, plus static memory of secure coding standards and vulnerability knowledge; retrieved examples and guidelines are injected into later reasoning.
framework_agente: Reflection-Driven Control / Reflex module for secure code generation agents
benchmarks_utilizados: 125 secure-code evaluation scenarios across eight classes of security-critical programming tasks
metricas_desempenho: Security rate, pass rate, effective total score, secure count, unresolved count, retrieval similarity, retrieval success rate, fallback usage rate, cost and token usage
baseline_comparado: Base models without Reflex versus Base+Reflex across gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-4o, qwen3-coder-plus, and gemini-2.5-pro
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Dynamic memory grows as case metadata and structured reflection records are written back after safe and repaired unsafe generations; retrieval performance evolves over iterations.
custo_por_tarefa: Average cost per scenario $5.37e-4; average cost per successful repair $5.67e-4; total token usage 4.48e4 tokens over 125 scenarios.
reducao_tokens: NR; paper reports minimal runtime and token overhead rather than token reduction.
resultado_principal: Reflection-Driven Control improves security and policy compliance while largely preserving functional correctness. Security rate gains include gpt-4o from 85.7 to 95.0, qwen3-coder-plus from 83.7 to 94.9, and gemini-2.5-pro from 88.0 to 97.1.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Base+Reflex improves security rate by +2.9 for gpt-3.5-turbo, +9.3 for gpt-4o, +11.2 for qwen3-coder-plus, and +9.1 for gemini-2.5-pro; pass rate is mostly preserved but decreases for qwen3-coder-plus (86.7 to 80.1).
custo_vs_baseline: Reports total cost $6.71e-2 for 125 scenarios, average $5.37e-4 per scenario, average $5.67e-4 per successful repair, and API price $1.5e-3 per 1K tokens; no direct baseline monetary comparison extracted.
limitacoes: Secure-code-generation setting; dynamic memory depends on reflection/verdict quality; qwen3-coder-plus pass rate decreases in the reported summary; source availability is not captured.
ameacas_validade: Scenario construction, vulnerability-class coverage, model selection, verifier quality, LLM reflection reliability, and retrieval fallback thresholds may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes Self-Defining Systems as an AI-agent workflow for infrastructure/software-system design, including a persistent agent-authored deployment playbook distilled from prior failures and reused on fresh VM deployments; it reports quantitative case-study results for two LLM runtime implementations, throughput, development time, and deployment-time reduction.

titulo: Self-Defining Systems

framework_agente: Custom Self-Defining Systems workflow for LLM-runtime development
llm_utilizado: NR for the coding agents; Qwen3-0.6B is used as the runtime benchmark workload.
benchmarks_utilizados: LLM runtime throughput benchmark on one NVIDIA L4 GPU in a GCP VM; nano-vLLM baseline; deployment-time case study.
metricas_desempenho: Throughput tokens/s, speedup since inception, development time, deployment time, human intervention.
baseline_comparado: nano-vLLM throughput, original Allmos baseline, naive PyTorch baseline, and human-in-the-loop deployment debugging.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: NA
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary cost; reports development and deployment time.
resultado_principal: allmos v2 reaches about 1.7k tokens/s, near nano-vLLM at 1.76k tokens/s and 76x faster than the original Allmos baseline. A monolithic runtime reaches 1.2k tokens/s and 53x speedup. An agent-authored key-solutions playbook reduces fresh-VM deployment from about 90 minutes of human-in-the-loop debugging to about 6 minutes without human intervention, and development time drops from 35 days to 2 days for the second runtime.
desempenho_vs_baseline: 76x speedup over original Allmos for allmos v2; 53x speedup for monolithic-llm-runtime since inception; deployment time about 90 minutes to about 6 minutes.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports time-efficiency gains rather than monetary cost.
limitacoes: White paper and ongoing case study; no controlled multi-system benchmark of SDS as a persistence architecture; agents did not exceed nano-vLLM performance and plateaued on some optimization attempts.
ameacas_validade: Single infrastructure domain, prototype workflow, substantial human framing of goals/evaluation, and no independent replication.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes Spark as a persistent shared experiential memory for coding agents, where structured experience traces are merged into a continuously evolving memory and evaluated on software-development tasks.
extractor_notes: Exact-title prior extraction `tablan2025smarter` reused after search text/title/path check; architecture_status=arch_shared_memory. Controlled-comparison status remains prior-prefilled pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Valentin Tablan, Scott Taylor, Gabriel Hurtado, Kristoffer Bernhem, Anders Uhrenholt, Gabriele Farei, Karo Moilanen
titulo: Smarter Together: Creating Agentic Communities of Practice through Shared Experiential Learning
formato_armazenamento: Knowledge base: ~34000 documentation blobs de bibliotecas data science (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, SciPy, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow) indexados como embeddings + texto. Experiential memory: traces estruturados de interações agente-usuário (contexto do problema, recomendação, feedback), clusterizados por similaridade semântica e curados em padrões generalizáveis. Aprendizado contínuo via epochs de curação.
framework_agente: custom (Spark memory layer, integrado via MCP)
benchmarks_utilizados: DS-1000 (1000 problemas de data science de StackOverflow: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, SciPy, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow)
metricas_desempenho: Code Quality Score (1-5, LLM judge: Gemini 2.5 Pro), Helpfulness Score (1-5, LLM judge: Claude Sonnet 3.7)
baseline_comparado: NO-SPARK (LLMs sem memória externa), DS-1000 Human Reference solutions (4.28/5 média)
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Inicializada com ~34K blobs de documentação; cresce via epochs de aprendizado experiencial (1 epoch avaliada neste estudo)
custo_por_tarefa: NR
resultado_principal: Spark melhora qualidade de código em todos os modelos: Qwen3-Coder +0.66 (4.23→4.89), Haiku 4.5 +0.41 (4.50→4.91), GPT-5-Codex +0.05 (4.78→4.83). Modelo open-weights 30B com Spark iguala GPT-5-Codex sem Spark. 98.2% das recomendações classificadas como pelo menos GOOD.
desempenho_vs_baseline: +0.66 code quality Qwen3-Coder (4.23→4.89); +0.41 Haiku 4.5 (4.50→4.91); +0.05 GPT-5-Codex (4.78→4.83); todos WITH-SPARK excedem qualidade humana (4.28)
custo_vs_baseline: NR
limitacoes: Dados experienciais sintéticos (gerados por GPT-4o, não interações reais); avaliação por LLM-judge sem métricas de execução (Pass@1); DS-1000 como único benchmark (data science); apenas 1 epoch de aprendizado experiencial avaliada
ameacas_validade: LLM-as-judge pode ter correlações com modelos avaliados; experiências sintéticas não representam interações reais; DS-1000 foca data science não SE geral (bug fixing, repository-level); modelos mais fortes beneficiam menos (ceiling effect); Spark é produto comercial, potencial viés

contextual_role: Benchmark evidence for continual learning and semantic memory evaluation in coding agents.
full_text_rationale: Full text introduces SWE-Bench-CL as a continual-learning benchmark and evaluation framework for coding agents with semantic memory, measuring experience accumulation and transfer across tasks.
extractor_notes: Rechecked prior extraction against search inclusion role; benchmark contextual record.
autores: Thomas Joshi, Shayan Chowdhury, Fatih Uysal
titulo: SWE-Bench-CL: Continual Learning for Coding Agents
formato_armazenamento: FAISS vector index armazenando embeddings de sumários de tentativas de tarefas (problema, solução, rationale, uso de ferramentas, status de sucesso). Embeddings: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small ou nomic-embed-text. Top-k retrieved e prepended ao prompt.
framework_agente: swe_agent (LangGraph-based, inspirado em SWE-agent, com ACI: Navigation/Search, File Viewing, Editing, Execution)
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-Bench-CL (273 tarefas, 8 repositórios Python, ordenadas cronologicamente com curriculum learning)
metricas_desempenho: ACC, F (forgetting), FT (forward transfer), BWT (backward transfer), AULC, TUE (tool-use efficiency), CL-Score, CL-F_β, Pass rate
metricas_cl: ACC, F, FT, BWT, AULC, TUE, CL-Score, CL-F_β (este paper DEFINE as métricas CL para agentes de codificação)
baseline_comparado: memory-enabled vs memory-disabled agents (protocolo proposto); harness SWE-bench padrão (preliminar)
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Cresce linearmente com tarefas processadas; sem mecanismo de compactação ou esquecimento
custo_por_tarefa: NR
resultado_principal: Propõe SWE-Bench-CL, primeiro benchmark de aprendizado contínuo para agentes de codificação (273 tarefas, 8 repos). Define suite de métricas CL (ACC, F, FT, BWT, AULC, TUE, CL-F_β). Análise preliminar revela baixa similaridade inter-tarefa (Jaccard 0.11, cosine 0.18) e alta sensibilidade a prompt poisoning. Harness SWE-bench é incompatível com avaliação CL.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Preliminar inconclusivo: pass rates <8.5% com e sem memória; condição memory-enabled não melhora (garbage-in, garbage-out por incompatibilidade do harness)
custo_vs_baseline: NR
limitacoes: Apenas 8 repositórios Python com até 50 issues; dificuldade estimada por tempo de correção humana (subjetivo); sequência estática com curriculum ordering não reflete dinâmica real; experimentos completos são propostos mas não realizados
ameacas_validade: Benchmark limitado a Python; curriculum ordering artificial; métricas CL propostas ainda não validadas empiricamente em escala; harness SWE-bench incompatível com SWE-Bench-CL; modelos preliminares são relativamente fracos (7-13B)

full_text_rationale: Full text describes SWE-Exp as an issue-resolution system that distills successful and failed prior repair trajectories into an evolving experience bank and reuses them across later SWE-bench issues.
extractor_notes: Exact-title prior extraction `chen2025sweexp` reused after search text/title/path check; architecture_status=arch_experience_bank. Controlled-comparison status remains prior-prefilled pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Silin Chen, Shaoxin Lin, Yuling Shi, Heng Lian, Xiaodong Gu, Longfei Yun, Dong Chen, Lin Cao, Jiyang Liu, Nu Xia, Qianxiang Wang
titulo: SWE-Exp: Experience-Driven Software Issue Resolution
formato_armazenamento: Multi-facet Experience Bank em vector database (embeddings Multilingual-E5-Large): cada experiência é dicionário com issue_type (label descritivo, e.g. VariableReferenceError), description (condições e cenários), e perspective ou modification (insights abstratos). Comprehension experiences guiam diagnóstico (perspectiva do problema); modification experiences guiam edição segura de código (estratégias de reparo). Extração offline via Experiencer agent de trajetórias successful e failed.
framework_agente: custom (estende SWE-Search com MCTS)
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-Bench Verified (500)
metricas_desempenho: Pass@1 (%), Average Token Costs, Average API Costs ($), Average Wall Time
baseline_comparado: Agentless, SWE-Agent, SWE-Search, OpenHands, EvoCoder, SAGE
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Fixo após construção offline do experience bank; saturação de ganhos em ~300 experiências (Figure 4b); performance platô com 1-2pp de variação além desse ponto
custo_por_tarefa: $0.13 avg per issue (DeepSeek-V3-0324)
resultado_principal: SWE-Exp atinge 73.0% Pass@1 com Claude 4 Sonnet e 42.0% com DeepSeek-V3-0324 no SWE-bench Verified, superando todos baselines. Comprehension e modification experiences contribuem independentemente (+3.2% e +2.6%); extração de experiências é o componente mais crítico (-6.0% sem ela).
desempenho_vs_baseline: +2.2pp vs SWE-Search com Claude 4 Sonnet (73.0% vs 70.8%); +6.6pp vs SWE-Search com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 35.4%); +3.2pp vs SWE-Agent com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.8%); +4.2pp vs EvoCoder com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.0%)
custo_vs_baseline: $0.13 vs $0.12 per issue com DeepSeek (+8.3%); wall time 15min49s vs 12min37s (+25%); retrieval+rerank overhead 37.5s avg
limitacoes: Eficácia depende da qualidade das experiências extraídas; k>1 experiências degradam performance (42.0%→39.0% com k=4); avaliação apenas em projetos Python; Instructor pode se tornar over-dependent de experiências mesmo quando exploração direta é suficiente
ameacas_validade: Modelo único por configuração (DeepSeek ou Claude 4 Sonnet); SWE-bench pode ter overlap com dados de treinamento do LLM; temporal leakage mitigada mas residual (excluem same-repo + post-target); avaliação apenas em Python; avaliação de qualidade da experiência em subset de 75 instâncias

full_text_rationale: Full text describes TAI3 maintaining and updating a datatype-aware strategy memory of successful mutation patterns, retrieving/adapting them for new seed tasks, with evaluation over 80 toolkit APIs.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 006; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Shiwei Feng, Xiangzhe Xu, Xuan Chen, Kaiyuan Zhang, Syed Yusuf Ahmed, Zian Su, Mingwei Zheng, Xiangyu Zhang
titulo: TAI3: Testing Agent Integrity in Interpreting User Intent
formato_armazenamento: Datatype-aware strategy memory stores successful mutation strategies indexed by parameter datatype and integrity category; novel strategies are added after an LLM duplicate check and top strategies are adapted for later seed tasks.
framework_agente: TAI3 API-centric stress-testing framework for LLM agents
llm_utilizado: GPT-4o-mini as default testing LLM, phi4-mini for prioritization, Llama-3.1-8B, Qwen3-30B-A3B, Claude-3.5-Haiku, Gemini-2.5-Pro, GPT-o3-mini and other target models
benchmarks_utilizados: 80 toolkit APIs, 233 parameters, five domains: Finance, Healthcare, Smart Home, Logistics, and Office
metricas_desempenho: Error-Exposing Success Rate (EESR), Average Queries to First Failure (AQFF), per-domain/category/model deltas
baseline_comparado: SelfRef baseline and ablations/generalization settings with different testing and target models
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Strategy memory grows as successful mutation strategies are added when non-duplicative; no long-run memory-size curve is reported.
custo_por_tarefa: NR
reducao_tokens: NR; reports query-efficiency through AQFF rather than token reduction.
resultado_principal: TAI3 consistently outperforms the SelfRef baseline in EESR across domains and input categories. In the VALID category it improves EESR by up to 15.5 points in Finance for Llama-3.1-8B and by 10.0 points in Office for GPT-4o-mini.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Table 1 reports broad EESR gains over SelfRef, including Finance GPT-4o-mini VALID 41.5 to 61.0 (+19.5), Healthcare GPT-4o-mini INVALID 44.7 to 57.4 (+12.7), and Office Llama-3.1-8B UNDERSPEC 65.7 to 82.0 (+16.3).
custo_vs_baseline: TAI3 reduces AQFF by up to 12% in the UNDERSPEC setting, indicating fewer target-agent queries to expose the first failure; no monetary cost is reported.
limitacoes: Focuses on intent-integrity testing rather than production coding assistance; no monetary/token cost data; memory stores mutation strategies rather than software-repair knowledge.
ameacas_validade: Toolkit/API selection, domain coverage, generated task realism, target-model selection, LLM duplicate judgments, and query-budget settings may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes TALM as a code-generation framework with a long-term memory module that stores, retrieves, and updates prior problem-solving experiences for future tasks, with HumanEval, BigCodeBench, and ClassEval evaluation.
extractor_notes: Exact-title prior extraction `shen2025talm` reused after search text/title/path check; architecture_status=arch_experience_bank. Controlled-comparison status remains prior-prefilled pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Ming-Tung Shen, Yuh-Jzer Joung
titulo: TALM: Dynamic Tree-Structured Multi-Agent Framework with Long-Term Memory for Scalable Code Generation
formato_armazenamento: Vector database com entries contendo: task description, reasoning process, generated code, e tree depth metadata. Consolidação automática: entries com similaridade > 0.95 são merged pelo LLM em entry representativa unificada (shared problem descriptions + solution patterns + code); originais removidas para evitar crescimento descontrolado. Retrieval: top-3 ANN search filtrado por tree depth.
framework_agente: custom (TALM)
benchmarks_utilizados: HumanEval (164), BigCodeBench (1140), ClassEval (100)
metricas_desempenho: Pass@1
baseline_comparado: Direct Prompting, Chain-of-Thought (CoT), Self-Planning Prompting, Reflexion, MapCoder
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: NR (significativamente menor que MapCoder conforme Figure 4)
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Cresce com tarefas resolvidas; consolidação merge reduz redundância (entries com sim > 0.95 fundidas); ganhos emergem após ~30 tarefas (Figure 5)
custo_por_tarefa: NR
reducao_tokens: ~50% menos tokens que MapCoder em ClassEval (Figure 4); overhead marginal com memória habilitada vs sem
resultado_principal: TALM com memória atinge 92.55% HumanEval, 53.27% BigCodeBench e 38.00% ClassEval (class level) com GPT-4o, superando todos baselines. Memória contribui mais em tarefas complexas (BigCodeBench +2.14pp, ClassEval class +2.00pp). Decomposição estruturada em árvore com re-reasoning localizado complementa acumulação de experiência.
desempenho_vs_baseline: +3.17pp BigCodeBench vs Direct (53.27% vs 50.10% GPT-4o); +5.00pp ClassEval class vs Direct (38.00% vs 33.00%); +2.00pp ClassEval class vs MapCoder (38.00% vs 36.00%); ~50% menos tokens que MapCoder
custo_vs_baseline: ~50% menos tokens totais que MapCoder em ClassEval; overhead marginal de memória vs TALM sem memória
limitacoes: Datasets relativamente pequenos (HumanEval 164, ClassEval 100); memória reiniciada a cada round experimental; apenas GPT-4o-mini e GPT-4o testados; apenas geração function/class-level, sem avaliação em repositórios reais (SWE-bench); hyperparameters fixos
ameacas_validade: Apenas modelos OpenAI (GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4o); HumanEval/ClassEval são function/class-level não representativos de SE real; memória reset entre rounds não reflete acumulação contínua; comparação com MapCoder re-implementado pode diferir do original; ClassEval com 100 tasks pode ter alta variância

full_text_rationale: Full text describes ToM-SWE maintaining a persistent user mental-state memory across multiple software-engineering sessions and evaluates it on stateful and ambiguous SWE benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Exact-title prior extraction `zhou2025tomswe` reused after search text/title/path check; architecture_status=arch_user_model_memory. Controlled-comparison status remains prior-prefilled pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Xuhui Zhou, Valerie Chen, Zora Zhiruo Wang, Graham Neubig, Maarten Sap, Xingyao Wang
titulo: TOM-SWE: User Mental Modeling For Software Engineering Agents
formato_armazenamento: Three-tier hierarchical memory como database externo: (1) Raw session storage — históricos completos de sessões anteriores como texto, (2) Session-based user models — JSON estruturado com user_intent, user_profile, message_preferences (inferred constraints, preferred_approach) extraídos via LLM analyze_session, (3) Overall user model — JSON agregado com profile_description, interaction_style (verbosity, question_timing), coding_preferences list, session_summaries. BM25 search across all tiers.
framework_agente: openhands
llm_utilizado: Claude Sonnet 4 (20250514), Claude 3.7 Sonnet, Qwen3-480B, GPT-5, GPT-5-mini, GPT-5-nano
benchmarks_utilizados: Ambiguous SWE-bench (500 instâncias), Stateful SWE benchmark (500 instâncias, custom com 15 perfis de desenvolvedor), In-the-wild human study (209 sessões, 174 sugestões ToM, 17 desenvolvedores profissionais, 3 semanas)
metricas_desempenho: Task resolved rate (%), User simulator satisfaction score (1-5 em 5 dimensões), ToM suggestion acceptance rate (%)
baseline_comparado: CodeAct (vanilla sem memória), RAGCodeAct (single-agent com RAG de sessões anteriores)
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Cresce com sessões: raw sessions acumulam; session user models consolidados em overall user model via LLM aggregation
custo_por_tarefa: $0.02 (GPT-5-nano ToM) a $0.17 (Claude Sonnet 4 ToM) overhead por sessão; ~16% do custo total SWE ($1.08 avg)
resultado_principal: ToM-SWE com Claude Sonnet 4 atinge 63.4% no Ambiguous SWE-bench (+11.5pp vs CodeAct) e 59.7% no Stateful SWE (+41.6pp vs CodeAct 18.1%). User satisfaction 3.62 vs 2.57 (+41%). Estudo in-the-wild com 17 devs profissionais: 86.2% aceitação de sugestões ToM (74.1% accept + 12.1% partial).
desempenho_vs_baseline: +11.5pp Ambiguous SWE-bench (63.4% vs 51.9%) com Claude Sonnet 4; +41.6pp Stateful SWE (59.7% vs 18.1%); satisfaction 3.62 vs 2.57 (+41%); RAGCodeAct inferior ao ToMCodeAct em todos modelos
custo_vs_baseline: $0.17 overhead ToM por sessão (~16% do custo total); GPT-5-nano ToM ($0.02) atinge 38.0% resolve rate no Stateful SWE; custo marginal para ganhos substanciais
limitacoes: User simulator (GPT-5) pode introduzir vieses sistemáticos (over-compliance, over-knowledgeability); 15 perfis derivados de OpenHands sessions podem não representar diversidade global de desenvolvedores; estudo humano observacional sem grupo controle; sessões reais não coletadas por privacidade
ameacas_validade: LLM-as-user-simulator pode não representar comportamento humano real; perfis baseados em desenvolvedores English-speaking usando OpenHands; correlação satisfaction-resolution (r=0.74) baseada em simulator scores; Stateful SWE benchmark novo sem validação externa; single-agent ToM testado limitadamente (N=100)

full_text_rationale: Full text describes HxAgent using short-term memory plus long-term experience from correct and incorrect action sequences, with a training/evaluation phase and empirical web-test generation evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Duy Cao, Phu Nguyen, Vy Le, Tien N. Nguyen, Vu Nguyen
titulo: Toward Generation of Test Cases from Task Descriptions via History-aware Planning
formato_armazenamento: HxAgent maintains short-term sequence-action memory as JSON/text state-action traces and long-term experience containing correct and incorrect action sequences selected from training for later evaluation prompts.
framework_agente: HxAgent history-aware planning agent for web test generation
llm_utilizado: GPT-4o; baseline comparisons include PaLM and other prior systems
benchmarks_utilizados: MiniWoB++ shared tasks, 125 challenging MiniWoB++ task instances, and 350 real-world web-application task instances
metricas_desempenho: Exact-Match accuracy, Prefix-Match accuracy, correct runs, ablation accuracy, prompt/output token consumption
baseline_comparado: WebGUM, RCI, Synapse, Li et al. staged LLM baseline with GPT-4o, and ablations without experience, without sequence-action memory, and without iterative planning
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: Average 800,000 prompt tokens per task/application across training and evaluation phases.
tokens_saida_por_tarefa: Average 22,000 output tokens per task/application across training and evaluation phases.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Experience starts empty during training, is updated from correct and incorrect sequences, and the selected optimal experience is fixed for evaluation; short-term memory records the action trace within each episode.
custo_por_tarefa: No monetary cost reported; average token consumption is 800,000 prompt tokens and 22,000 output tokens.
resultado_principal: HxAgent reaches 97% Exact-Match and 99% Prefix-Match on MiniWoB++, 82% Exact-Match on 125 challenging MiniWoB++ task instances, and 87% Exact-Match / 93% Prefix-Match on 350 real-world web-application task instances.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On challenging MiniWoB++ tasks, HxAgent averages 82% Exact-Match versus 10% for Li et al. (GPT-4o). On real-world applications it averages 87% Exact-Match versus 28% and 93% Prefix-Match versus 34%. Removing experience drops Exact-Match from 97% to 88%; removing both SAM and experience drops it to 47%.
custo_vs_baseline: NR as direct baseline cost; token consumption is reported but not compared against baseline token use.
limitacoes: Long-term experience is represented simply as correct/incorrect examples; date pickers, animations, pop-ups, modals, loading screens, and dynamic web elements remain limitations; token use is high.
ameacas_validade: MiniWoB++ and selected real-world web tasks may not generalize to all web applications; baseline replication, task-instance selection, and GPT-4o prompting affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes Xolver as a multi-agent reasoning framework with persistent holistic experience memory and an evolving shared memory that accumulates reasoning paths, solutions, and feedback across programming/math benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Exact-title prior extraction `hosain2025xolver` reused after search text/title/path check; architecture_status=arch_experience_bank. Controlled-comparison status remains prior-prefilled pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Md Tanzib Hosain, Salman Rahman, Md Kishor Morol, Md Rizwan Parvez
titulo: Xolver: Multi-Agent Reasoning with Holistic Experience Learning Just Like an Olympiad Team
formato_armazenamento: Dual memory: (1) Episodic memory D_E — corpus externo de problemas passados com reasoning traces e soluções (OpenMathReason para math, cp-algorithms C++ para código), recuperável via BM25 ou self-retrieval paramétrico do LLM; atualizado com cada problema resolvido (Xolver+). (2) Intermediate shared memory D_S — buffer dinâmico de tamanho fixo (|D_S|=m) contendo top-m tuplas (reasoning trace T, response R, feedback (T_S, s), agent a) ranqueadas por score s do Judge agent; evolui a cada iteração intra-problema.
framework_agente: custom (Xolver)
benchmarks_utilizados: GSM8K, AIME 2024 (30 problemas), AIME 2025 (30 problemas), Math-500, LiveCodeBench v5
metricas_desempenho: Accuracy (%), Pass@1
baseline_comparado: LongCoT (backbone direto), Self-Reflexion, OctoTools, Search-o1, CheatSheet, CodeSim; modelos proprietários (o1, o3, o4-mini-high, Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash); open-weight (DeepSeek-R1, Qwen3-235B); especializados (rStar-Math, AlphaOne, OpenMathReason, OpenCodeReason)
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: ~45-135k tokens totais por problema (input+think+output combinados, varia por dataset e backbone; Figure 8)
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: D_E cresce monotonicamente com Xolver(+) cross-problem updates; D_S é buffer fixo de tamanho m per-problem (reseta entre problemas); ganho médio de +3.5pp com episodic memory dinâmica
custo_por_tarefa: NR
reducao_tokens: NR (~1.5× tokens vs Search-o1 single-agent, mas com ganhos substanciais de performance)
resultado_principal: Xolver com o3-mini-medium atinge 97.1% GSM8K, 93.8% AIME'24, 89.4% AIME'25, 99.2% Math-500 e 87.3% LiveCodeBench, superando consistentemente todos baselines incluindo frameworks especializados. Com o3-mini-high, estabelece novos SOTA: 98.1% GSM8K, 94.4% AIME'24, 93.7% AIME'25, 99.8% Math-500, 91.6% LiveCodeBench. Memória episódica cross-problem contribui +3.5pp em média.
desempenho_vs_baseline: +18.6pp AIME'24 avg vs o3-mini-medium LongCoT (93.8% vs 75.8%); +13.6pp AIME'25 (89.4% vs 75.8%); +21.0pp LiveCodeBench (87.3% vs 66.3%); supera o4-mini-high em AIME'24 (93.8% vs 93.4%) e LiveCodeBench (87.3% vs 69.5%)
custo_vs_baseline: ~1.5× tokens vs Search-o1; complexidade de update O(mI) vs O(n²) do CheatSheet; runtime parallelizável entre agentes
limitacoes: Custo computacional elevado (~1.5× tokens de single-agent); dependente da qualidade do backbone LLM; retrieval corpus externo em C++ para tarefas Python (cross-language); self-judging inferior a external judging (-9.9% code, -3.88% math); foco em math e competitive programming, sem avaliação em SE real
ameacas_validade: Benchmarks de math e competitive programming podem não generalizar para SE real; 16-32 runs para variância mas sem confidence intervals formais; retrieval corpus curado (OpenMathReason, cp-algorithms) pode não representar qualidade de retrieval em outros domínios; comparação com modelos proprietários usa resultados reportados oficialmente

full_text_rationale: Full text describes AdaExplore building cross-task memory of validity rules from execution failures for Triton/kernel generation and evaluating runtime optimization on KernelBench.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Weihua Du, Jingming Zhuo, Yixin Dong, Andre He, Weiwei Sun, Zeyu Zheng, Manupa Karunaratne, Ivan Fox, Tim Dettmers, Tianqi Chen, Yiming Yang, Sean Welleck
titulo: AdaExplore: Failure-Driven Adaptation and Diversity-Preserving Search for Efficient Kernel Generation
formato_armazenamento: Cross-task skill memory of validity rules distilled from recurring execution failures on synthesized Triton/kernel tasks, plus local working memory from recent search-path states and a representative kernel pool as progress storage.
framework_agente: AdaExplore failure-driven adaptation and diversity-preserving kernel search agent
llm_utilizado: GPT-5-mini as default multi-pass model; GPT-5, Claude-4.6-Opus, Qwen3-Coder-Next in comparisons
benchmarks_utilizados: KernelBench Level-2 and Level-3, FlashInfer-Bench, TritonBench-T transfer checks
metricas_desempenho: Accuracy, speedup over PyTorch eager baseline, Fast@1.2, Fast@2, pass rates with and without skill memory, ablation speedup
baseline_comparado: Single-pass GPT-5-mini/GPT-5/Claude-4.6-Opus, AutoTriton, DR. Kernel, parallel sampling, iterative refinement, OpenEvolve, variants with cross-task skill memory, and AdaExplore ablations
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: From 200 synthesized tasks, AdaExplore collects 1,178 raw experiences, deduplicates to 174 unique experiences, and retains 78 high-frequency memory items.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; inference budgets are reported in steps rather than monetary/token cost.
resultado_principal: AdaExplore reaches 100% accuracy and 3.12x speedup on KernelBench Level-2 at 100 steps, and 100% accuracy and 1.72x speedup on Level-3. At 50 steps it reaches 2.65x Level-2 speedup and 1.55x Level-3 speedup.
desempenho_vs_baseline: At 50 steps on Level-2, AdaExplore reaches 2.65x speedup versus IR with skill memory at 2.59x and PS with skill memory at 2.12x. Removing cross-task skill memory drops speedup from 2.65x to 2.32x and Fast@1.2 from 71% to 56%.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; increased compute from 50 to 100 and 200 steps improves speedup, but no monetary or token-cost baseline is extracted.
limitacoes: Kernel-generation focus in Triton and GPU-runtime optimization; skill memory can bias some search strategies toward conservative updates; no cost/token reporting captured.
ameacas_validade: KernelBench task mix, GPU hardware, timing methodology, synthetic task generation, memory filtering thresholds, and model/backbone choices may affect generalization.

full_text_rationale: Full text proposes AgentDevel, an externalized release-engineering pipeline for agent improvement that persists execution traces, diagnostics, release candidates, promoted versions, and regression gates, with quantitative evaluation on SWE-bench Lite/Verified and other execution-heavy benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Di Zhang
titulo: AgentDevel: Reframing Self-Evolving LLM Agents as Release Engineering
formato_armazenamento: Versioned release-engineering artifacts for agents: execution traces, rubric/programmatic scores, symptom labels, diagnostic scripts, release candidates, promoted blueprint versions, flip lists, and regression-gate decisions.
framework_agente: AgentDevel release-engineering pipeline for self-evolving LLM agents
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-bench Lite, SWE-bench Verified, WebArena, StableToolBench
metricas_desempenho: Resolved rate, success rate, SoWR, fail-to-pass flips, pass-to-fail regressions, regression rate, hit rate, bad release count
baseline_comparado: Initial base agent, SWE-agent, GPT-4o scaffolded baseline, CER_hybrid, DFS, and ablations without flip gate, without executable diagnosis, and with non-blind critic
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Maintains a single canonical version line; each iteration preserves current version, release candidate evidence, accepted/rejected gates, and promoted official versions until stopping criteria trigger.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; paper reports non-trivial compute and wall-clock overhead but no per-task monetary/token cost in extracted evidence.
resultado_principal: AgentDevel doubles SWE-bench Lite resolved rate from 11.0% to 22.0% and SWE-bench Verified from 15.0% to 30.0%. It also improves WebArena from 17.0% to 35.5% and StableToolBench SoWR from 54.0% to 73.5%.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE-bench Lite, AgentDevel reaches 22.0% versus base 11.0% and reported SWE-agent 18.0%. On WebArena ablation, the full pipeline keeps P2F regression rate at 3.1% with zero bad releases; removing the flip gate raises P2F to 14.8% and creates four bad releases.
custo_vs_baseline: Qualitatively higher overhead than single-pass or in-agent refinement due to iterative execution, trace collection, diagnostic scripts, and gating; no exact cost ratio extracted.
limitacoes: Pipeline incurs non-trivial overhead; LLM critic and diagnostic stages can inherit model inconsistencies; may be less suitable for rapid prototyping or highly dynamic environments.
ameacas_validade: Single-agent/single-environment settings, benchmark selection, programmatic scorer availability, release-gate thresholds, and LLM critic reliability may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes AgentGA launching fresh autonomous code-generation runs with inherited parent archives that persist reusable artifacts across generations, with Weco-Kaggle Lite and parent-child lineage evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: David Y. Y. Tan, Kellie Chin, Jingxian Zhang
titulo: AgentGA: Evolving Code Solutions in Agent-Seed Space
formato_armazenamento: Population-level archive of autonomous run artifacts, parent solutions, experiments, agent chains, and code artifacts; selected parent archives are copied into a Previous Experiments directory for child runs while each child starts from a fresh workspace.
framework_agente: AGENT GA agent-seed genetic algorithm with LangGraph-based autonomous code-generation runs
benchmarks_utilizados: 10 of 16 Weco-Kaggle Lite tabular AutoML competitions, Kaggle private leaderboard submissions, 1,135 parent-child tournaments
metricas_desempenho: Exceeds percent of human competitors, Kaggle rank, parent-child tournament win/loss share, median relative gain, prompt/completion/cached token trace for example run
baseline_comparado: AIDE benchmark reference; contextual comparisons to H2O AutoML, AutoGPT, and human with ChatGPT; Initial de novo proposals versus parent-conditioned operators
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: Example invocation consumed 1.87M prompt tokens including 1.56M cached prompt tokens.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Archive persists every iteration, run, agent chain, parent solution, and experiment artifact across generations; operators choose zero, one, or two parent archives plus optional reference archives.
custo_por_tarefa: No monetary cost reported; example invocation took 56 steps, 15 min 10 s, 1.87M prompt tokens, and 34,252 completion tokens.
resultado_principal: Across 10 completed Weco-Kaggle Lite benchmark runs, AGENT GA averages 74.52% Exceeds % of Human versus 54.15% for AIDE, a +20.36 point mean improvement. All 10 completed runs beat the AIDE private-leaderboard reference.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Parent-conditioned operators win 48.7% of parent-child tournaments overall, while Initial proposals win only 12.2%. Continue wins 148/286 tournaments, Merge 68/104, EDA 267/593, Ablation 34/78, and Initial 9/74.
custo_vs_baseline: Long-horizon agents consume substantially more tokens and wall-clock time than artifact-centric generation; no direct monetary baseline comparison is reported.
limitacoes: Only 10 of 16 benchmark runs are reported; long-horizon compute cost is high; seed quality is observed only indirectly through stochastic descendants; parent archives are optional context and can be consumed inconsistently.
ameacas_validade: Partial benchmark coverage, stochastic trajectories, Kaggle task/domain focus, multi-split evaluation choices, tournament operator allocation, and unpublished full artifacts may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text proposes APEX-EM, a non-parametric online-learning framework with a procedural-episodic Experience Memory and Procedural Knowledge Graph that accumulates, retrieves, and reuses structured task executions across tasks, with evaluation on BigCodeBench and other benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Pratyay Banerjee, Masud Moshtaghi, Ankit Chadha
titulo: APEX-EM: Non-Parametric Online Learning for Autonomous Agents via Structured Procedural-Episodic Experience Replay
formato_armazenamento: Persistent Experience Memory organized as a Procedural Knowledge Graph with structured procedural-episodic traces, entities, plans, generated artifacts, iteration histories, error analyses, quality scores, and dual successful/failed outcome indexing.
framework_agente: APEX-EM Plan-Retrieve-Generate-Iterate-Ingest workflow
llm_utilizado: Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Opus 4.5, GPT-4o; embeddings via Amazon Titan in extracted setup
benchmarks_utilizados: BigCodeBench, KGQAGen-10k, Humanity's Last Exam; planned/comparative MemRL benchmarks include Lifelong Agent Bench and ALFWorld
metricas_desempenho: Last Epoch Success Rate, Cumulative Success Rate, LASM accuracy for KGQA, iteration-count reduction, transfer accuracy, ablation success rates
baseline_comparado: No Memory, Pass@10, Reflexion, RAG, Self-RAG, Mem0, MemP, MemRL, semantic-only/entity-graph/full-memory ablations, Opus-judge ablation
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Experience Memory begins empty and grows across epochs/tasks as quality-gated execution traces are committed to the Procedural Knowledge Graph; offline-to-online learning is also supported.
custo_por_tarefa: NR as monetary/token cost; reports iteration reduction from memory accumulation, such as BCB full memory 1.50 to 1.32 iterations and KGQA Opus judge 4.40 to 2.50 iterations from epoch 1 to epoch 10.
reducao_tokens: NR; memory reduces self-correction iterations by 11-17% on BCB and 37-43% on KGQA in Table 11.
resultado_principal: On BigCodeBench, APEX-EM with Opus judge reaches 83.3% SR / 84.0% CSR versus 53.9% no-memory baseline. On KGQAGen-10k it reaches 89.6% accuracy / 95.3% CSR, and on HLE entity graph retrieval reaches 48.0% / 53.3% CSR versus 25.2% no-memory baseline.
desempenho_vs_baseline: BigCodeBench improvement is +29.4pp over no memory and exceeds MemRL's reported +11.0pp gain. KGQA full-memory plus Opus judge reaches 89.6% versus 41.3% no memory. HLE entity graph retrieval improves 25.2% to 48.0%.
custo_vs_baseline: Memory reduces average iterations from epoch 1 to epoch 10: BCB reductions of 11-17% and KGQA reductions of 37-43%, but no monetary/token baseline is reported.
limitacoes: Some experiments remain in progress; direct comparisons use different backbones from MemRL; plan compression and learned retrieval policies are future work; remaining MemRL benchmark evaluations are not yet complete.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark/backbone mismatch, LLM judge quality, memory namespace isolation, epoch count, retrieval thresholds, and transfer-split construction may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text proposes BEAM, a bi-level LLM-powered heuristic/code design system with Adaptive Memory storing reusable generated function components and a knowledge-augmentation pipeline, evaluated across combinatorial and continuous optimization benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Chuyang Xiang, Yichen Wei, Jiale Ma, Handing Wang, Junchi Yan
titulo: BEAM: Bi-level Memory-adaptive Algorithmic Evolution for LLM-Powered Heuristic Design
venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems / arXiv preprint (2604.12898)
formato_armazenamento: Adaptive Memory stores high-performing generated function components with names/descriptions so future algorithm structures can import and recombine them; Knowledge Augmentation also uses HeuBase component repositories and KnoBase text knowledge.
framework_agente: BEAM bi-level memory-adaptive algorithmic evolution framework
benchmarks_utilizados: TSP, BPP, CAF, MIS, CVRP, BBOB, PMSP and related heuristic-design benchmarks
metricas_desempenho: Optimality gap, objective value, runtime/time or token budget, average and variance of final gaps, token-consumption trajectory
baseline_comparado: EoH, ReEvo, MCTS-AHD, AlphaEvolve, LLaMEA, LLaMEA-HPO, KaMIS, RLSA, ARW, HGS, Split and local search, EACO-EDM, BE without Adaptive Memory, one-shot and MCTS education baselines
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: NR; token budgets and token-consumption trajectories are reported for some experiments, including 55w token CVRP budget.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Adaptive Memory is updated during evolutionary resets with high-quality generated functions; HeuBase is intentionally compact and designed to grow through community contribution.
custo_por_tarefa: NR as monetary cost; experiments report time budgets from 18 minutes to 5 hours and token-budget experiments up to 55w tokens.
reducao_tokens: Qualitative: Adaptive Memory lets the LLM call stored functions instead of regenerating long code blocks, shrinking emitted code; no exact token-reduction percentage extracted.
resultado_principal: BEAM reduces the optimality gap by 37.84% in aggregate on CVRP hybrid algorithm design and generates competitive or superior heuristics across MIS, CVRP, TSP, BBOB, and other optimization benchmarks.
desempenho_vs_baseline: In CVRP, BEAM with Split and local search reports gaps of 0.09%, 0.38%, and 0.86% for CVRP-100/200/500 versus EoH gaps of 0.22%, 0.57%, and 1.09%. In BBOB, BEAM average gap is 0.007 versus ReEvo 0.957, EoH 6.032, and LLaMEA-HPO 0.386.
custo_vs_baseline: BEAM needs more tokens before generating its first heuristic in complex tasks due to the bi-level structure, but Adaptive Memory is intended to reduce repeated code emission; no monetary baseline is reported.
limitacoes: Bi-level structure can require larger initial token consumption; framework may add unnecessary complexity for single-function generation; some comparisons use repository best heuristics with unknown budgets.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark heterogeneity, budget matching, unreproduced baseline results, LLM/model differences, token/time budget settings, and code-execution failures in initial generations may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes PatchAdvisor using layered retrieval-based memory from historical Linux kernel patch evolution to guide later repair/review generation, with temporally held-out evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Luyao Bai, Kenan Alghythee, Hang Zhang, Xiaoguang Wang
titulo: Beyond Crash-to-Patch: Patch Evolution for Linux Kernel Repair
formato_armazenamento: Three-layer PatchAdvisor memory from syzbot-linked patch evolution: instance memory with crash evidence, fix characterization, final merged diff, and patch-evolution summary; pattern memory with FixStrategy and ReviewLesson records; embedding memory over crash reports and patch artifacts for semantic retrieval.
framework_agente: PatchAdvisor repair framework with retrieval memory and fine-tuned diagnostic advisor
llm_utilizado: Claude coding model, Gemma-3-12B advisor, LLaMA-3.2-11B advisor
benchmarks_utilizados: 6,946 syzbot-linked Linux kernel bug-fix lifecycles, 100 temporally held-out crash cases for diagnostic evaluation, and six recent syzbot bugs for end-to-end repair
metricas_desempenho: Diagnostic Coverage Rate, precision, recall, F1, fix rate, CodeBERTScore, compilation success rate, average AI-assessed quality rank
baseline_comparado: Unguided baseline coding agent, memory-guided agent, base advisors without fine-tuning, fine-tuned PatchAdvisor variants
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory is constructed from 6,946 reconstructed syzbot-fixed bugs and can grow as additional patch-evolution lifecycles are collected; end-to-end test excludes held-out recent bugs from memory/training.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; advisor fine-tuning used LoRA on one NVIDIA A100 80GB GPU, but no monetary/token cost per task is reported.
resultado_principal: PatchAdvisor improves reviewer-alignment and repair quality. Memory raises diagnostic F1 by 21.8% (0.330 vs 0.271), and end-to-end memory guidance raises repair success from 1/6 to 4/6; the fine-tuned Gemma-3-12B advisor reaches 5/6 fixes, CodeBERTScore 0.91, and 100% compilation success.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Baseline repair succeeds on 1/6 bugs with CodeBERTScore 0.42 and 83.3% compile success. Memory-guided repair succeeds on 4/6 with CodeBERTScore 0.74. Fine-tuned Gemma-3-12B PatchAdvisor succeeds on 5/6 with average rank 2.7.
custo_vs_baseline: NR
limitacoes: End-to-end repair evaluation uses only six recent bugs after strict selection; dataset is scoped to syzbot/Linux kernel workflows; advisor gives diagnostic summaries rather than complete patches.
ameacas_validade: Temporal holdout, syzbot workflow focus, keyword-mapped reviewer categories, small end-to-end bug sample, AI-assessed patch ranking, and compiler/toolchain differences may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes Co-RedTeam using layered long-term memory for reusable vulnerability patterns, attack strategies, and technical actions in autonomous software vulnerability discovery and exploitation, with CyBench and BountyBench evaluation plus memory-role analysis.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Pengfei He, Ash Fox, Lesly Miculicich, Stefan Friedli, Daniel Fabian, Burak Gokturk, Jiliang Tang, Chen-Yu Lee, Tomas Pfister, Long T. Le
titulo: Co-RedTeam: Orchestrated Security Discovery and Exploitation with LLM Agents
formato_armazenamento: Layered long-term memory with Vulnerability Pattern Memory, Strategy Memory, and Technical Action Memory; successful and failed trajectories are distilled into reusable conceptual, strategic, and concrete action memories.
framework_agente: Co-RedTeam multi-agent vulnerability discovery and exploitation framework
llm_utilizado: Gemini-2.5-flash, Gemini-2.5-pro, Gemini-3-pro, gemini-embedding-001, gemini-2.5-pro for memory synthesis
benchmarks_utilizados: CyBench, BountyBench, CyberGym
metricas_desempenho: Attack success rate, exploit success, detection accuracy, precision/recall for detection, ablation success rates, average latency
baseline_comparado: Vanilla prompting, OpenHands, C-Agent, VulTrail, RepoAudit, component ablations without critique, validation, vulnerability documents, code browser, memory, or execution
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory may be warm-started from curated security items or cold-started empty, then updated sequentially as tasks are processed and successful practices or failure lessons are written back.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary/token cost; average latency is reported, e.g. Gemini-3-pro Co-RedTeam 319.8s on CyBench, 198.7s on BountyBench, and 605.2s on CyberGym.
resultado_principal: With Gemini-3-pro, Co-RedTeam achieves 63.7% CyBench ASR, 65.0% BountyBench exploit success, 20.0% BountyBench detection accuracy, and 37.3% CyberGym ASR, outperforming all reported baselines.
desempenho_vs_baseline: For Gemini-3-pro, OpenHands reaches 45.2%/45.0%/5.0%/20.2% across CyBench, BountyBench exploit/detect, and CyberGym; Co-RedTeam reaches 63.7%/65.0%/20.0%/37.3%. Removing memory drops Gemini-2.5-pro CyBench from 59.1% to 50.0%, BountyBench exploit from 60.0% to 40.0%, and CyberGym from 31.5% to 22.6%.
custo_vs_baseline: Latency is lower than OpenHands and C-Agent in reported comparisons, e.g. 198.7s versus OpenHands 219.6s on BountyBench with Gemini-3-pro; no monetary cost baseline reported.
limitacoes: Security red-team domain focus; memory initialization may affect early performance; multi-agent orchestration depends on execution environments and benchmark-specific validation.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark task mix, curated memory warm start, LLM/model family, iteration budgets, evaluator definitions, and tool/environment realism may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes Codebase-Memory as a persistent Tree-Sitter knowledge graph exposed to coding agents through MCP, with repository/question-answering and SWE-bench-related evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Martin Vogel, Falk Meyer-Eschenbach, Severin Kohler, Elias Grünewald, Felix Balzer
titulo: Codebase-Memory: Tree-Sitter-Based Knowledge Graphs for LLM Code Exploration via MCP
formato_armazenamento: Persistent Tree-Sitter-based codebase knowledge graph stored in a single SQLite file, maintained incrementally through file watching/content hashes, and exposed through 14 typed MCP structural-query tools.
framework_agente: Codebase-Memory MCP server used by MCP-compatible coding agents
llm_utilizado: Claude Opus 4.6 for head-to-head evaluation; compatible with Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Zed, VS Code, and other MCP clients
benchmarks_utilizados: 31 real-world repositories across 31 languages; 12 structural code-question categories; system performance benchmarks on Apple M3 Pro
metricas_desempenho: Answer quality score, tool calls per question, tokens per question, query latency, indexing time, incremental re-index time
baseline_comparado: File-exploration agent using conventional file reading and grep; comparison to embedding/RAG, RepoMap, and graph-plus-LLM retrieval approaches
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: Approximately 1,000 tokens per question for MCP Agent versus approximately 10,000 for Explorer Agent.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Graph persists across sessions and updates incrementally when source files change; Linux kernel indexing reports 2.1M nodes and 4.9M edges.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary cost; token/tool-call reductions are reported and translate to lower latency/cost.
reducao_tokens: 10 times fewer tokens per question and 2.1 times fewer tool calls than Explorer Agent in Table 6.
resultado_principal: Codebase-Memory achieves 0.83 answer-quality score versus 0.92 for an Explorer Agent, while using about 1,000 versus 10,000 tokens per question, 2.3 versus 4.8 tool calls, and sub-millisecond query latency.
desempenho_vs_baseline: MCP Agent reaches 90% of Explorer quality while using 10 times fewer tokens and 2.1 times fewer tool calls; it matches or exceeds explorer behavior on graph-native hub/caller queries for 19 of 31 languages.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports direct efficiency gains: about 10x fewer tokens, 2.1x fewer tool calls, and query latency under 1 ms versus 10-30 s for file exploration.
limitacoes: Graph intentionally omits line-level source context and macro-heavy C performs weakly; dynamic dispatch is limited; benchmark uses one LLM backend and first-author grading.
ameacas_validade: Single LLM backend, manual reference grading, repository/language sample, abstraction limits of Tree-Sitter/graph representation, and single hardware platform for performance benchmarks may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes CoEvoSkills constructing persistent multi-file skill packages for Claude Code and Codex, accumulating verifier feedback across iterations and transferring evolved reusable task structures to fresh coding-agent runs.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Hanrong Zhang, Shicheng Fan, Henry Peng Zou, Yankai Chen, Zhenting Wang, Jiayu Zhou, Chengze Li, Wei-Chieh Huang, Yifei Yao, Kening Zheng, Xue Liu, Xiaoxiao Li, Philip S. Yu
titulo: CoEvoSkills: Self-Evolving Agent Skills via Co-Evolutionary Verification
formato_armazenamento: Persistent structured multi-file skill packages with SKILL.md, instructions, scripts, reusable utility functions, and verifier-derived improvement notes; skills are installed for fresh Claude Code/Codex agents and updated across evolution rounds.
framework_agente: CoEvoSkills co-evolutionary skill-generation framework for Claude Code and Codex
llm_utilizado: Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.2, Claude Code, Codex, six additional transfer LLMs
benchmarks_utilizados: SkillsBench: 87 tasks across roughly 20 professional domains and 11 reported domain groups
metricas_desempenho: Pass rate, delta versus no-skill/human-curated baselines, cross-model transfer pass rate, per-domain pass rate, verification-cycle counts, ablation pass rate
baseline_comparado: No-skill baseline, Anthropic Skill-Creator, single-session skill generation, SkillsBench self-generated skills, CoT-guided self-generation, human-curated skills, ablations without surrogate verifier or without evolution
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Skill versions evolve for up to five rounds; persistent skills are written back as reusable packages and transferred to fresh agents and other model families.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; paper states convergence within five rounds keeps evolution cost practical and reports average verification-cycle counts, but no monetary/token cost per task is extracted.
resultado_principal: CoEvoSkills reaches 71.1% pass rate on SkillsBench with Claude Opus 4.6 + Claude Code, versus 30.6% no-skill baseline and 53.5% human-curated skills. It also reports GPT-5.2 self-evolved skills at 69.8% and broad cross-model transfer gains of about 36-44pp.
desempenho_vs_baseline: CoEvoSkills improves +40.5pp over no-skill and +17.6pp over human-curated skills. Skill-Creator reaches 34.1%, single-session generation 32.4%, SkillsBench self-generated 32.0%, and CoT-guided self-generation 30.7%. Removing the surrogate verifier drops pass rate from 71.1% to 41.1%; removing evolution drops to 48.6%.
custo_vs_baseline: NR as monetary/token cost; evolution converges by about round 5 and requires verifier/oracle cycles.
limitacoes: Ablation rows are single runs due to computational cost; skill evolution depends on verifier quality; results are SkillsBench-centered; generated skills may be domain/model sensitive despite transfer gains.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark composition, deterministic oracle tests, surrogate-verifier information isolation, model/harness choices, number of evolution rounds, and computational budget may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text proposes Complementary RL with a MemoryManager and evolving experience bank that distills, merges, retrieves, and reuses structured experience across tasks, with quantitative evaluation on SWE-Bench and other environments.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Dilxat Muhtar, Jiashun Liu, Wei Gao, Weixun Wang, Shaopan Xiong, Ju Huang, Siran Yang, Wenbo Su, Jiamang Wang, Ling Pan, Bo Zheng
titulo: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
formato_armazenamento: Evolving experience bank managed by a centralized MemoryManager; the experience extractor distills, merges, deduplicates, retrieves, and maintains structured experience entries while co-evolving with the policy actor.
framework_agente: Complementary RL in the ROLL training framework
benchmarks_utilizados: MiniHack Room, WebShop, ALFWorld, SWE-Bench Verified, multi-task mixtures
metricas_desempenho: Success rate, validation score, number of actions per task, multi-task evaluation average, performance gain versus baseline
baseline_comparado: Outcome-based RL baseline without experience, Static Online Experience, Static Online Experience evaluated without experience, Experience Only, Complementary RL with/without experience at evaluation
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: NR; training configuration includes maximum sequence lengths up to 32,768 for actor and 65,536 for experience extractor.
tokens_saida_por_tarefa: Maximum output tokens per step are configured as 4,096 for several experiments and 2,048 for ALFWorld.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Experience bank is updated asynchronously during RL training through MemoryManager operations, periodic merges, semantic deduplication, and retrieval; static/offline variants use fixed banks for ablation.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary cost; reports action-count efficiency such as 1.5x fewer actions on MiniHack and 2x fewer actions on ALFWorld.
resultado_principal: Complementary RL consistently outperforms no-experience baselines. In single-task training it reports about a 1.3x performance margin on MiniHack Room and ALFWorld and a +3.0% gain on SWE-Bench; in multi-task evaluation, Complementary RL with experience averages 0.82 versus 0.75 baseline.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Multi-task table reports baseline average 0.75, Complementary RL evaluated with experience 0.82, and Complementary RL without experience 0.78. Static Online Experience with experience averages 0.59 and without experience 0.54, showing that static experience alone is insufficient.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports behavioral efficiency rather than monetary cost: 1.5x fewer actions on MiniHack Room and 2x fewer actions on ALFWorld; no direct SWE-Bench cost reduction is reported.
limitacoes: SWE-Bench training uses a curated subset of 124 tasks because many full-dataset tasks are too difficult for smaller models; static experience baselines underperform due to distributional misalignment.
ameacas_validade: Curated task selection, training hyperparameters, Qwen model family, experience extractor size, retrieval/deduplication design, and multi-task contamination may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text proposes Controlled Self-Evolution for algorithmic code optimization with Hierarchical Evolution Memory, including local intra-task lessons and global cross-task experience templates retrieved from a vector database, evaluated on EffiBench-X.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Tu Hu, Ronghao Chen, Shuo Zhang, Jianghao Yin, Mou Xiao Feng, Jingping Liu, Shaolei Zhang, Andy Wang, Wenqi Jiang, Yuqi Fang, Sen Hu, Huacan Wang, Yi Xu
titulo: Controlled Self-Evolution for Algorithmic Code Optimization
formato_armazenamento: Hierarchical Evolution Memory with local intra-task memory of improvement/failure lessons and global cross-task memory of compact experience items distilled from top improving/degrading evolution steps; entries are embedded with Qwen3-8B-Embedding and retrieved by cosine similarity.
framework_agente: Controlled Self-Evolution (CSE) for algorithmic code optimization
benchmarks_utilizados: EffiBench-X with 623 algorithmic problems in Python and C++
metricas_desempenho: Execution-time ratio, peak-memory ratio, memory-integral ratio versus human solutions, best-so-far MI, improvement count, iteration at best, last-10 improvement count, ablation metrics
baseline_comparado: Direct generation, Self-Reflection, SE-Agent, AlphaEvolve, ablations without diversified planning, genetic evolution, or hierarchical memory
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Local memory is compressed whenever it exceeds 1,000 tokens; for each processed task, top improving and degrading steps are distilled into global memory and added for subsequent task retrieval.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; fixed test-time budget of 30 candidates per task and no monetary/token cost per task reported.
resultado_principal: CSE consistently outperforms baselines on EffiBench-X across four LLM backbones. With GPT-5, CSE averages ET 67.70%, MP 65.62%, and MI 67.47%; with Claude-4.5-Sonnet, it averages ET 73.23%, MP 70.11%, and MI 74.41%.
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-5, CSE improves average MI to 67.47% versus Direct 59.13%, Self-Reflection 60.24%, SE-Agent 64.41%, and AlphaEvolve 63.04%. Ablation on GPT-5 shows full CSE MI 68.10% versus 63.08% without memory, 63.82% without planning, and 64.72% without evolution.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; reports higher efficiency within the fixed 30-candidate budget but no monetary or token-cost comparison.
limitacoes: Does not yet distill CSE trajectories into model-weight RL training; multi-round evolution still incurs inference cost; results are algorithmic optimization specific.
ameacas_validade: EffiBench-X benchmark composition, candidate budget, fallback to Direct when budget exhausts, model/backbone selection, memory construction order, and embedding retrieval settings may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes CORAL using shared persistent memory where agents retrieve, contribute, and distill notes and reusable skills across asynchronous open-ended optimization tasks, including systems/kernel optimization evaluation and memory ablations.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 009; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Ao Qu, Handi Zheng, Zijian Zhou, Yihao Yan, Yihong Tang, S. Y. Ong, Fenglu Hong, Kai-Qing Zhou, Chonghe Jiang, Minwei Kong, Jiacheng Zhu, Xuan Jiang, Sirui Li, Cathy Wu, Bryan Kian Hsiang Low, Jinhua Zhao, P. Liang
titulo: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
formato_armazenamento: Shared persistent filesystem under .coral/public with attempts as JSON records, notes as Markdown files with YAML front matter, reusable skills as SKILL.md plus scripts, heartbeat configuration, session state, and private evaluator state symlinked into isolated agent worktrees.
framework_agente: CORAL autonomous multi-agent evolution infrastructure with CLI, shared hub, isolated worktrees, and heartbeat prompts
llm_utilizado: Claude Opus 4.6 via Claude Code; MiniMax M2.5 with OpenCode
benchmarks_utilizados: Six mathematical optimization tasks, five systems optimization tasks, Anthropic kernel engineering, Polyominoes packing, VLIW SIMD tree traversal, and related stress-test optimization tasks
metricas_desempenho: Final score, improvement rate, number of evaluations, cycle count, coverage, local-test rate, attempt inspection, knowledge created per attempt, knowledge access rate, read-to-improvement rate
metricas_cl: Knowledge-created rate, knowledge-access rate, and read-to-improvement rate over agent trajectories
baseline_comparado: OpenEvolve, ShinkaEvolve, EvoX, single-agent CORAL, independent best-of-four single-agent runs, no-knowledge ablation
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory grows through attempts, notes, and skills written by agents; advanced tasks create 0.55 to 0.68 reusable knowledge entries per attempt versus 0.05 on standard tasks; Kernel Engineering run reports 663 total attempts in artifact examples.
custo_por_tarefa: Typical 3-hour single-agent run on a mathematical optimization task with Claude Opus 4.6 costs approximately USD 30-60; four-agent runs cost approximately 3-4 times a single-agent run.
resultado_principal: Single-agent CORAL achieves the best final score on all 11 mathematical and systems tasks and establishes new SOTA on eight tasks. Four-agent co-evolution further improves stress-test results, including Kernel Engineering from 1350 cycles to 1103 cycles.
desempenho_vs_baseline: CORAL improvement rates are reported as 3-10 times higher than fixed evolutionary-search baselines and typically converge within 5-20 evaluations versus 60-100 for baselines. Knowledge ablation worsens Kernel Engineering from 1350 to 1601 cycles, Polyominoes from 80.2 to 77.3, and Txn Scheduling from 4566 to 4444.
custo_vs_baseline: CORAL uses fewer external evaluation calls than structured baselines within the same wall-clock budget, but LLM API calls dominate cost and four-agent runs multiply API cost by about 3-4 times.
limitacoes: Relies on frontier foundation models, lacks bootstrapped agent heterogeneity, assumes a reasonably specified evaluator, and multi-agent runs increase API cost.
ameacas_validade: Task-suite composition, hidden or stochastic evaluators, trajectory classification by rule-based and LLM-based analysis, model/runtime choice, and convergence-based stress-test budgets may affect validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text describes DARWIN using persistent JSON memory files to track prior reasoning, file hierarchy, code changes, and summaries across self-improvement generations of training-code optimization, with benchmarked MFU/perplexity outcomes and a memory ablation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 009; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Henry Jiang
titulo: DARWIN: Dynamic Agentically Rewriting Self-Improving Network
formato_armazenamento: Persistent JSON-based memory files record evolutionary history, source file paths, code-modification summaries, timestamps, benchmark outcomes, token counts, backend metadata, file hierarchy, and previous structure across generations.
framework_agente: DARWIN evolutionary self-improvement loop over nanoGPT training-code agents with human-in-the-loop request interface
benchmarks_utilizados: nanoGPT on a Shakespeare prose dataset
metricas_desempenho: Best perplexity, mean perplexity, standard deviation perplexity, best MFU, mean MFU, generation time, training errors, resolved errors, error rate, error-resolution rate
baseline_comparado: Baseline nanoGPT configuration and ablation without the memory file system
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: One persistent memory record is generated for each mutation event, including modification summary, file path, timestamp, token counts, backend metadata, and benchmark-correlated results for later generations.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; reports average time per generation of 223 seconds but no monetary cost.
resultado_principal: After five generations, DARWIN reports best perplexity 37.70 versus baseline 38.498 and best MFU 0.392 versus baseline 0.397; the paper describes the overall result as not very promising and not a significant improvement.
desempenho_vs_baseline: The paper reports a 2.07 percent reduction in perplexity and a 1.26 percent reduction in MFU; removing persistent memory produced about 3 percent worse performance, but the authors caution that the results are not statistically meaningful.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; reports runtime per generation and resource-scaling concerns but no token or monetary comparison.
limitacoes: Proof-of-concept on a simple nanoGPT/Shakespeare task, few generations, insignificant results, manual safety checks, and scaling to GPT-2 or larger models would require substantial compute.
ameacas_validade: Small task and model, optimized baseline, only five generations, high mutation probability, simple training code, manual intervention/safety checks, and limited compute constrain validity.

full_text_rationale: Full text studies experience-augmented agents with raw, lesson-distilled, consolidated, and concept-tree memory built from past interactions; it includes a SWE-agent/SWE-bench setting using stored debugging experiences and evaluates retrieval/consolidation effects on SWE-bench Verified.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 009; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Maohao Shen, Kaiwen Zha, Zexue He, Zhang-Wei Hong, Siru Ouyang, J. Ryu, P. Sattigeri, Suhas N. Diggavi, Gregory W. Wornell
titulo: Decocted Experience Improves Test-Time Inference in LLM Agents
formato_armazenamento: Raw memory stores problem statements, sampled agent trajectories, and feedback; distilled lesson memory stores reusable lessons; consolidated memory keeps k-means representatives; hierarchical concept trees organize lesson memories into coarse-to-fine concept groups for retrieval.
framework_agente: Experience-augmented agent with SWE-agent for software-engineering tasks
benchmarks_utilizados: Math reasoning benchmarks, WebShop, SWE-bench full training set excluding SWE-bench Verified, and SWE-bench Verified
metricas_desempenho: Average accuracy, average output tokens, average input tokens, average interaction steps, retrieval relevance, SWE-bench resolved rate
metricas_cl: Performance as accumulated experience size grows, memory-consolidation sweet spot, concept-tree diversity and retrieval quality
baseline_comparado: Vanilla agent without context, raw experience retrieval, distilled lesson retrieval, full memory, k-means consolidated memory, flat top-k lesson retrieval, hierarchical concept tree
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: Figures report average input-token curves up to roughly 300k tokens depending on task and K, but no per-task scalar is tabulated.
tokens_saida_por_tarefa: Figures report average output-token or interaction-step curves; no per-task scalar output-token table is provided.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: SWE memory construction uses 1,794 training instances from SWE-bench full excluding Verified, four sampled trajectories per instance, and evaluates on 500 SWE-bench Verified instances; concept-tree appendix reports 579 SWE experience records organized into 8 broad topics and 38 concrete concept groups.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; paper discusses that interaction can increase cost and uses output tokens or interaction steps as efficiency measures.
reducao_tokens: Lesson distillation provides strong performance with smaller input contexts than raw experience and can prevent degradation from excessively long noisy prompts; exact reduction is plotted rather than tabulated.
resultado_principal: Across math, WebShop, and SWE settings, both raw and distilled experience outperform vanilla agents; distilled lessons outperform raw experience on agentic WebShop and SWE tasks, memory consolidation shows a non-monotonic sweet spot, and concept-tree retrieval improves context construction over flat lesson retrieval on WebShop and SWE.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On WebShop and SWE, lesson-based context consistently outperforms raw trajectories and vanilla context-free agents. Concept-tree retrieval improves over top-k lesson retrieval on WebShop and shows a noticeable improvement on SWE, while excessive K/raw context can degrade performance.
custo_vs_baseline: Efficiency is reported through generated output tokens, input-token scaling curves, and interaction steps rather than monetary cost; distilled lessons need fewer input tokens for comparable performance than raw trajectories.
